$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `doc_mount_points` repo — the WIDEST
mount-index sibling-DB repo of Phase 2 (enums, a boolean, two JSON arrays,
three REAL-int counters, nullable strings/timestamp). One committed file
drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-doc-mount-points-fixture.ts + doc-mount-points-tier2.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness doc_mount_points tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-mount-index.db (NOT the main
DB). The builder/oracle point SQLITE_MOUNT_INDEX_PATH at the fixture and read
it back through getRawMountIndexDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that
file by path. Mirrors the group_character_members pilot's machinery exactly.
FRESH-FIXTURE NOTE: v4's getCollection() runtime ALTER-TABLE migrations
(totalSizeBytes, conversionStatus, conversionError, storeType) are NO-OPS on a
fresh fixture — generateDDL already emits all four columns, so every migration
guard is false. The fixture therefore has EXACTLY the 18 schema columns.
ALL FIELDS SET EXPLICITLY on every create so v4's Zod .default(...) never
fires and both sides insert identical bytes.
AFFINITY: fileCount/chunkCount/totalSizeBytes are z.number().int() with NO
min&max → REAL by v4's mapToSQLiteType (bound f64). The canonical dump
collapses integer-valued REAL back to a JSON integer, so 0/5/etc. match v4.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.
BANKED across the corpus: enabled both 0 and 1; a nullable column both null
and non-null; REAL-int counts both 0 and >0; arrays non-empty and empty.

ops.comment: Add an Obsidian mount; all fields explicit (no Zod default fires). enabled=1, counts>0, non-empty arrays, lastScannedAt set, errors null.
Re-enable seed row 2 (enabled false->true), bump counts 0->non-zero, set scanStatus scanning, set lastScannedAt (was null). createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Remove seed row 1 (the filesystem Vault mount).
